overview: Ojo de Agua is a vibrant city located in the heart of Mexico, part of the greater metropolitan area of Mexico City. With a population of approximately 386,290, it plays a significant role in the cultural and economic landscape of the region. Known for its rich history and diverse community, Ojo de Agua serves as a key hub for commerce and tourism, attracting visitors with its unique blend of modern amenities and traditional Mexican charm.
history: Ojo de Agua was established in the early 20th century, primarily as a settlement for agricultural workers. Its growth was fueled by its proximity to Mexico City, evolving into a key residential and commercial area.
economy: The economy of Ojo de Agua is diverse, with key industries including manufacturing, retail, and services. The city's strategic location near Mexico City enhances its role as a commercial center, fostering growth and attracting businesses that contribute to the local economy.
tourism: Tourism in Ojo de Agua is characterized by its cultural attractions and local festivals. Visitors can explore beautiful parks, historical sites, and vibrant markets, making it an ideal destination for those looking to experience authentic Mexican culture and hospitality.

best_restaurants.name: El Fogón
best_restaurants.description: A popular spot known for its delicious grilled meats and traditional Mexican dishes.

best_restaurants.name: La Casa de Toño
best_restaurants.description: Famous for its comforting pozole and authentic Mexican flavors.

best_restaurants.name: Café de Tacuba
best_restaurants.description: A historic café offering a delightful mix of traditional and contemporary Mexican cuisine.

best_restaurants.name: Pujol
best_restaurants.description: An upscale restaurant renowned for its innovative take on Mexican gastronomy.

best_restaurants.name: Tacos El Huequito
best_restaurants.description: A must-visit for taco lovers, famous for its flavorful tacos al pastor.

weather_summary: Ojo de Agua experiences a temperate climate with mild winters and warm summers, often accompanied by seasonal rainfall.

faqs.question: What time zone is Ojo de Agua in?
faqs.answer: Ojo de Agua is in the America/Mexico_City time zone.

faqs.question: Does Ojo de Agua observe daylight saving time?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ojo de Agua observes daylight saving time, typically starting in early April and ending in late October.

faqs.question: What is the population of Ojo de Agua?
faqs.answer: The population of Ojo de Agua is approximately 386,290 residents.

faqs.question: When does sunrise typically occur in Ojo de Agua?
faqs.answer: Sunrise in Ojo de Agua typically occurs around 6:30 AM, varying slightly throughout the year.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Ojo de Agua?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit Ojo de Agua is during the dry season, from November to April, when temperatures are comfortable.
